componentDescription: KeyValuePair displays a labeled piece of data in a structured row. Use it in detail views, summary panels, and profile pages where you need to show field-value pairs consistently (e.g., 'Email: user@example.com', 'Status: Active'). Compose multiple KeyValuePairs for structured data displays.
features: Configurable label width for aligned multi-row displays
compositionPattern: Combine multiple KeyValuePairs in a vertical stack to create a data detail panel. For aligned layouts, set a consistent labelWidth across all pairs.

usageExamples.title: User detail panel
usageExamples.description: Multiple key-value pairs showing user account details.
usageExamples.code: } labelWidth={120} />

usageExamples.title: Order summary
usageExamples.description: Order details in a summary card.
usageExamples.code: Order #12345

label.llmContext: Required. The field name/label displayed on the left. Use consistent Title Case or sentence case across all pairs in a section. Keep labels concise (1-3 words).

value.llmContext: Required. The value to display. Can be a string, number, or React node (for Tags, Avatar, formatted content). When the value might be empty, provide a fallback like '—' or 'Not set'.
value.propDescription: Required. The value — string, number, or React node

labelWidth.llmContext: Set a consistent pixel width for the label column across multiple KeyValuePairs to create aligned columns. Choose a width that accommodates your longest label. E.g., if the longest label is 'Member since' (~110px), set labelWidth={130} for all pairs.
labelWidth.propDescription: Fixed width for the label column to enable alignment

variant.llmContext: Controls the visual style of the label-value layout. Use 'horizontal' for side-by-side label and value on the same row. Use 'vertical' (stacked) when labels are long or the container is narrow.
